Track 2: Align AI companion to framework + add action awareness

- Rewrite chat system prompt: framework-aligned tone (Part 4), depth level
  parameterised via DEPTH_LEVEL_INSTRUCTIONS, sub-slider refs removed,
  active actions context block added
- Update analysis prompt: add extracted_actions (habit/completable with
  domain and confidence) and language_quality (precision, honesty,
  avoidance_level) to JSON output format
- Wire action context into chat service: _format_active_actions() queries
  active actions with consistency % for habits and mention count for
  completables; passed to system prompt; extracted_actions included in
  SSE done event; depth level resolved from user preferences
- Align V2 companion prompt: framework tone, remove sub-slider references
  from TODAY'S ENTRY, add language_quality to JSON output

  context formatting and LLM call, simplify slider_scores to overall only

commit.message:
feat: complete JARVIS auto-restart watchdog — 7 services monitored

Watchdog now monitors ALL JARVIS services with auto-restart:
- LM Studio M1 (port 1234) + auto-reload qwen3-8b model
- Ollama (port 11434)
- Dashboard (port 8080)
- Canvas proxy (port 18800)
- JARVIS WS (port 9742)
- Gemini proxy (port 18791)
- Telegram bot (PID file check, depends on canvas proxy)

Full chain: Windows Startup → JARVIS_AUTOSTART.bat (infinite loop)
→ unified_boot.py --watch (6 phases + 60s watchdog cycle)
→ if crash, BAT relaunches after 15s

commit.message:
fix(setup): refresh state before retry to fix duplicate SG rule errors

When re-running setup.sh on an existing deployment, Terraform state can
drift from AWS reality (e.g. security group rules exist but state says
otherwise). The retry now runs `apply -refresh-only` first to sync state
before the corrective apply.

Add v0.53.0: 2D geometry completions — GccAna bisectors, line/circle solvers, IntAna2d, Extrema2d, Geom2dLProp, Bisector_BisecAna

- Geom2dLProp: curvature extrema (min/max) and inflection point detailed analysis

commit.message:
Remove per-category supply split; show all sources as unified pool in dashboard

Dashboard now shows two separate panels side by side:
LEFT - Demand breakdown: Domestic / Irrigation / Amenities with annual,
       monthly average, and % demand share columns
RIGHT - Supply sources (pooled, no category allocation):
        Borewells | RWH/Dugwell | STP Recycled | Greywater | Municipal/Tanker
        Total Supply = sum of all sources
        Net Balance bar below (green/red) = Total Supply - Total Demand

No bifurcation of which source serves which category. Each source input
contributes to one shared pool; gap is assessed at the total level only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix SQLite migration: use CREATE UNIQUE INDEX instead of UNIQUE column constraint

SQLite does not support ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN with UNIQUE. Split into
two steps: add column then create unique index separately.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(layout): suppressHydrationWarning on body for browser extension attrs

Browser extensions (1Password, Bitwarden, etc.) inject attributes like
cz-shortcut-listen='true' into <body> before React hydration, causing
a false hydration mismatch. suppressHydrationWarning silences it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Redesign Bestie creation: purpose-first flow, backgrounds, Easter eggs

- Purpose drives everything: suggested backgrounds, avatar highlights, default style
- Step 2: Name, avatar (with diverse skin tones, professions), language

Add workflow validation V4 report and MCP server walkthrough

V4 validates the pipeline across 3 missions (fizz-buzz, roman-numerals,
cron-engine) using gh commands. Key findings: simple missions complete
in 1-2 iterations, only gpt-5-mini supports reasoning-effort, complex
missions still churn. MCP_SERVER.md gets a step-by-step walkthrough.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: cascading catalog dropdowns + seed path fix for systemd

- Replace single 3000-item dropdown with Model → Memory → Color cascade
- loadCatalogForCreate() now groups items and filters step by step
- createOfferCatalogId hidden input holds resolved catalog ID
- Catalog reloads each time user opens Create Offer screen
- database.py: DB_NAME now uses absolute path (works in systemd)
- _seed_catalog_if_empty: adds script dir to sys.path before import

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use proper vt100 snapshots for session-host reconnection (#97)

* fix: use vt100 state_formatted for session-host snapshots and wrap restored sessions with shell fallback

Two fixes for Claude terminal pane scrolling and session restoration:

1. Session-host snapshot now uses vt100's `state_formatted()` instead of
   a hand-rolled plain-text renderer. The old snapshot stripped all cell
   colors/attributes and terminal modes (mouse tracking, alternate screen,
   bracketed paste, cursor visibility). When the TUI reconnected, the local
   vt100 parser didn't know about mouse tracking, so scroll events were
   handled by Claustre's own scrollback instead of being forwarded to Claude
   Code — causing a behavioral mismatch and broken scrolling.

2. `restore_session_tab` now wraps the fallback `claude --continue` with
   `wrap_cmd_with_shell_fallback()`, matching what `create_session` does.
   Previously, when the session-host was gone and Claude was respawned
   directly, the PTY would die when Claude exited, leaving a frozen pane.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: make Claude pane a normal local PTY instead of session-host proxy

The Claude terminal pane now spawns directly as a local PTY — identical
to the shell pane — instead of going through the session-host Unix
socket intermediary. This eliminates the entire class of scrolling bugs
caused by the lossy snapshot/reconnection mechanism.

Changes:
- create_session returns the claude command instead of spawning a
  session-host; the TUI spawns it directly via EmbeddedTerminal::spawn
- restore_session_tab always spawns `claude --continue` as a local PTY
  wrapped with shell fallback (no socket connection attempt)
- reconnect_running_sessions scans DB for active sessions instead of
  scanning socket files
- Removed Remote backend variant, connect() method, and session-host
  shutdown logic from teardown
- Cleaned up protocol module: removed client-side functions (decode,
  encode, read/write helpers) that were only kept for tests
- session_host.rs is retained as a CLI subcommand but the TUI no
  longer uses it

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add AME-2 Direct Workflow (Isaac Lab DirectRLEnv)

New files:
- ame2_direct/: Isaac Lab DirectRLEnv implementation
  - config.py: AME2DirectEnvCfg (ANYmal-D, DCMotor, 48D obs, 12D action)
  - env.py: AME2DirectEnv + AME2DirectWrapper (all reward/obs/term inline)
- scripts/train_ame2_direct.py: PPO training with curriculum (outer loop + RSL-RL)
- scripts/play_direct.py: headless eval script

vs manager-based: ~3x faster (~15s/iter vs ~50s), no manager overhead

Reward fixes (critical):
- w_joint_pos_limits -1000→-1.0: HFE limits hit by noise_std=1.0+action_scale=0.5
  caused -300/ep vs +15 positive (reward signal destroyed)
- w_early_termination -500→-100: avoids value function collapse early training
- w_link_contact_forces 0.0: DCMotor excess.square() reward explosion
- lin_vel_tracking 1.5: Isaac Lab standard exp(-||cmd-vel||^2/0.25)

Training infra fixes:
- save_interval: outer loop saves model_{it}.pt every 25 iters
  (RSL-RL internal save broken when called with learn(num_iter=1))
- resume: detect it_start from checkpoint filename

Result: mean_reward -1533→+47 after fixes, model_25/50.pt saving confirmed

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(sdk): AI RPC fallback — remove AI binding requirement for all AI ops

- Add _aiApiCall() private helper (mirrors _storageApiCall): SSRF guard,
  operation allowlist, pre-flight auth check, 60s timeout
- chat/generate: fall back to /v1/ai/chat when no env.AI binding
- search.ingest/query: skip local embed+Vectorize when no _ai, route to
  /v1/ai/search/{ingest,query} so platform handles embedding server-side
- search.delete/deleteByType/listTypes/configure: switch from _rpcCall
  (internal hooks) to _aiApiCall (public RPC) — more reliable path
- search.update/get/count: add three missing methods
- embed: improved error message with aerostack.toml instructions
- Security: SSRF extended to block IPv6/RFC-1918; Content-Type only set
  for non-GET; auth pre-flight guard added; update() uses explicit
  destructure instead of object spread

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(payment): protéger le plan admin contre les webhooks Stripe

Un webhook customer.subscription.deleted ou invoice.payment_failed
pouvait downgrader silencieusement un compte is_admin=True vers
plan='free', coupant l'accès à toutes les fonctionnalités Pro.

Guard ajouté dans 3 endroits :
- _ensure_plan_from_subscription : if user.is_admin → return
- _downgrade_from_subscription   : if user.is_admin → return
- checkout.session.completed     : if not user.is_admin → update plan

Les admins conservent plan='pro' indépendamment de tout événement Stripe.

commit.message:
fix: update quick setup guide + detailed tutorial to match new LINE flow

- Quick guide: updated 5 steps with correct order (Provider → Channel → OA → Enable API → copy keys)
- Detailed guide: step 1 now includes Provider creation + SMS verification
- Detailed guide: step 2 now shows OA Manager → Messaging API flow
- Detailed guide: step 4 emphasizes auto-reply disable as critical step
- Changed step 4 warning from alert-warning to alert-danger for visibility

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: shell-safe commit message handling in review workflow

Multiline commit messages (with body + Co-Authored-By trailers) broke
the bash script because ${{ github.event.head_commit.message }} gets
template-substituted before the shell runs — newlines in the commit
message were interpreted as shell commands (exit code 127).

Fix: pass commit message, PR title, and PR number as env vars instead
of inline ${{ }} expressions. Use printf for safe multiline output
and a unique delimiter (COMMIT_DELIM) to avoid EOF collisions.

commit.message:
Add modem passthrough RTP settings to PJSIP trunk

The d-modem reference implementation explicitly disabled VAD and echo
cancellation (no_vad=true, ec_tail_len=0) which are critical for modem
audio. Add RTP timeout settings to prevent Asterisk from timing out
the media stream during modem training sequences.
